criteria_source: workflow contract hard gates plus gpt-5.5|xhigh baseline comparison

hard_gates.description: Output includes all required update, repo, skill-sync, material, junction, follow-up, boundary, and completion sections.
Candidate treats the update as event-driven and does not convert it into a morning scheduled workflow.
Public, _workmeta, and private-state repo freshness and allowed actions are separated.
Candidate syncs only tracked Codex skill bridges when policy allows and blocks ad hoc installed skill edits.
Candidate uses _workmeta/system/bindings/workspace_junctions.yaml as intent source, keeps host-local cloud roots out, and checks target suffix semantics.

Candidate classifies stale junctions, root mirrors, source-ledger gaps, and workspace creation gaps without auto-repair, source download, NotebookLM operation, secret inspection, or owner approval claims.
Candidate routes gaps to metadata-only downstream workflows or owner-decision surfaces without copying private/source payloads.
Candidate states that no real pull, skill sync, junction audit, repair, source download, or mutation occurred in the synthetic output.
Candidate preserves unavailable or unknown remote states as blockers or uncertainty and does not infer freshness.
Candidate treats junction target suffix matching as metadata-only review and does not emit host-local absolute target paths.
Candidate names downstream workflow or owner-decision surfaces for follow-up routes and states metadata-only handoff.

failure_conditions: claims real pull, skill sync, junction audit, repair, source download, or mutation occurred
stores or invents host-local absolute paths or cloud roots
uses a non-tracked installed skill mirror as sync authority
auto-repairs a stale junction or extra root mirror without authority
treats metadata gaps as source truth or owner approval
infers freshness for unknown or unavailable remote state
omits downstream workflow or owner-decision route names for follow-up gaps
mixes public, workmeta, and private-state repo states
quality_equivalence_rule: A candidate may pass with different JSON layout or wording if every hard gate is met, no critical boundary condition is lost, and the output is directly usable as a synthetic latest-update follow-up packet.
